placements.EncodedRooms: There's a 'pack template rooms to log' mod option. This will log a string. If you paste that string here, you can delete the original rooms if this template is in the room with the things using those rooms. Can be used between maps.

entities.auspicioushelper/FinderDepth.description: Change the depth of targeted entities

placements.path: A list of entity ID paths (the number next to ID: in loenn topbar)
placements.depth: The new depths these entities should have

entities.auspicioushelper/EntityMarkingF.name: Entity Marker
entities.auspicioushelper/EntityMarkingF.description: Finds an entity by ID and assigns it a string id. Add to a zztemplates room.

placements.identifier: The unique identifier to give the entity, when found

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateFiller.description: Switch between various template fillers. Should be placed in a template room and used like a normal template

placements.template_name: The filler name (analogous to regular template filler)
placements.channel: If using channel selection mode, selects the filler at the index of the channel's current value
placements.LoopResetMode: How to reset loop mode - i.e. whether to share the loops between all instances in a room or not
placements.templates: List of template strings to use.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateCasset.description: Set up custom visuals for a single cassette layer.

placements.simpleStyle: Whether to use reduced border style
placements.channels: Apply this visual setting to all listed channels
placements.fgSaturation: How saturated the foreground is (reduces tint influence)
placements.color: The base color. Setting this automatically sets all other color parameters to reasonable values if you leave them unset.
placements.patternScale: The size of each stripe in the pattern
placements.patternOffset: Offset of the pattern in worldspace
placements.patternWeight: How thick the 'active' bands are in the pattern

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateCasset.description: Manage template cassette block visuals and timings simply in one entity. Just give channels and provide the matching channels in the cassette blocks.

placements.visual_only: While setting up visuals on the listed channels, will not set the channels itself.
placements.tintActive: Whether to tint activated cassette blocks.
placements.translucent: Make the area between stripes see-through
placements.no_visuals: Change the channels along with vanilla cassette timings without setting up visuals
placements.useVanillaMistiming: Vanilla sometimes activates blocks the frame after the click plays. this replicates that.
placements.useListeners: Uses cassette listeners. Better for compatability.

entities.auspicioushelper/CustomDreamlay.description: Setup custom visual settings for tempalte dreamblocks. Global per map; does not need to be in same room.

placements.color_0: Specifies color of stars in the dreamblock
placements.color_1: Specifies color of stars in the dreamblock
placements.color_2: Specifies color of stars in the dreamblock
placements.color_3: Specifies color of stars in the dreamblock
placements.color_4: Specifies color of stars in the dreamblock
placements.color_5: Specifies color of stars in the dreamblock
placements.depth: Depth the dreamblocks will render at
placements.identifier: The identifier to use in 'custom dreamlayer' boxes. Set to 'normal' or 'reverse' to change the default graphics for dreamblock modifiers without an identifier.
placements.density: The density of stars in the dreamblock.
placements.contentAlpha: The alpha of the original entities.
placements.edgeColor: The color of the borders of the effect
placements.insideColor: The color of the interior of the dreamblock.

entities.auspicioushelper/templateFiller.name: Template Filler
entities.auspicioushelper/templateFiller.description: Place this entity so that it's covering your desired group of objects, and then it can be used as a template (make sure the room name starts with "zztemplates-")

placements.template_name: The name of this template. When using this template, specify it like <roomname>/<templatename>, assuming this template is placed in a room named "zztemplates-<roomname>"

entities.auspicioushelper/templateholdab.name: Template Holdable
entities.auspicioushelper/templateholdab.description: A holdable that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.cannot_hold_timer: How long one must wait before being allowed to pick up this entity after dropping it
placements.Holdable_collider_expand: How much the holdable collider should extend from the base. Can specify up to 4 options seperated with commas - specifying 1 will set all sides to that amount, 2 will set the top and bottom to the first and left and right to the second, etc...
placements.always_collidable: Whether this should collide with other actors even when it is being held (will not collide with the holder)
placements.player_momentum_weight: When grabbed, how much the final player velocity should use the player's velocity
placements.holdable_momentum_weight: When grabbed, how much the final player velocity should use the holdable's velocity
placements.wallhitsound: sound to play when hitting walls or floor
placements.wallhit_speedretain: When hitting walls, how much speed to retain
placements.gravity: How quickly to approach terminal velocity
placements.dontFlingOff: When liftspeed stops or becomes pointed downwards, whether this holdable should be flung
placements.start_floating: Yea like if it starts floating
placements.dangerous: Whether to crush things like hearts
placements.voidDieOffset: How far below the screen the bottom of the collider must be for this to die
placements.minHoldTimer: Minimum time this holdable must be held before becoming throwable
placements.customThrowspeeds: The speed this item is thrown at. Can give two values for X and Y.
placements.mirrorReflection: Whether this template has a mirror reflection.
placements.customRecoil: The recoil throwing this template causes. Can pass an X and Y value.

entities.auspicioushelper/MaterialTempla.description: A template that can be drawn to material layers

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.dontNormalRender: Don't render stuff in this template in the normal gameplay render step
identifier: The layer to draw entities in this template to
placements.collidable: If unchecked, items in the template will be purely visual
placements.channel: Render as normal when this is set

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateBehavi.description: An easy way to apply attributes from multiple template types to one template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.forceOwnPosition: Use this template's position as the root position of any noded templates. Good for more intuitive node layouts. The entity position of those templates will be completely ignored when checked.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateBlock.name: Template Breakable Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateBlock.description: A static block that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.only_redbubble_or_summit_launch: If the template can only be broken when in a red bubble or summit launching
placements.persistent: If the template will respawn after being broken
placements.canbreak: Whether or not you can dash into this template to break it
placements.breaksfx: The sound effect to play when this template is broken
placements.breakableByBlocks: Whether this block will be broken by templates with the 'through dashblocks' option
placements.visible: Whether the template is visible or not
placements.collidable: Whether the template is collidable or not
placements.active: Whether the template will update or not
placements.propagateRiding: Whether to pass player riders to parent template in compound templates
placements.propagateShaking: Whether shake this block when the parent shakes when in compound template

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateCasset.name: Template Cassette Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateCasset.description: A cassette block that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.channel: Will be present when channel is nonzero; also used for rendering.
placements.freeze: Whether or not this template's entities should update when inactive
placements.do_boost: bounces the template up when activates (jank)
placements.refreshEntsAlways: Every time the cassette spawns, will make new entities. The old ones get destroyed.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateChanne.name: Template Channelmover
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateChanne.description: A template that moves between two nodes based on a channel

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.channel: The channel to listen to. When this channel is active/odd, this template moves to the end node. (To use a flag, prefix the flag with a $)
placements.move_time: How long in seconds it takes to move from one node to the other
placements.asymmetry: What to divide the return speed by (ex. move_time=2 and asymmetry=4, return move speed is 0.5sec)
placements.easing: The easing to use along this line
placements.spline: The spline type to use. Linear types move in straight lines. The catmull variants curve smoothly between nodes with sharp edges at knots (created by stacking two nodes on the same pixel)
placements.lastNodeIsKnot: Make the last node a knot even if it is not stacked. Can be disabled to allow for crooked paths on the last segment of a looping path. Having it on results in more intuitive behavior for new users.
placements.allowFraction: Allows you to specify decimal targets to move to specific positions between two knots
placements.complete: Whether the template must complete its movement to a knot before reversing direction
placements.shake: Whether this will shake upon reaching a knot.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateBelt.description: Make a conveyer belt of entities

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.speed: The speed of items on the belt, in segments/second
placements.numPerSegment: How many items are spawned per segment in the belt naturally. More items can be spawned via setting the channel.
placements.initialOffset: The initial offset items in the belt should have.
placements.loop: whether to make the belt loop (will cause the same items to circulate endlessly instead of being destroyed/recreated)
placements.spline: The spline type to use. Linear types move in straight lines. The catmull variants curve smoothly between nodes with sharp edges at knots (created by stacking two nodes on the same pixel)
placements.lastNodeIsKnot: Make the last node a knot even if it is not stacked. Can be disabled to allow for crooked paths on the last segment of a looping path. Having it on results in more intuitive behavior for new users.
placements.channel: Spawn an item at the start of the belt every time the channel is set to a nonzero value.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateDashhi.description: Modify the behavior performed when dashing into the various sides of this block

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.skipChannel: Ignores this template when set, behaving in a manner as if it were not here
placements.Left: to send triggering events, pick the Trigger variety here
placements.Right: to send triggering events, pick the Trigger variety here
placements.Up: to send triggering events, pick the Trigger variety here
placements.Down: to send triggering events, pick the Trigger variety here
placements.refillOptions: give a number to restore that many dashes, and an 's' to restore stamina
placements.alwaysPropegate: Whether to send dashhits further down the chain after use

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateDreamb.description: Turn contained entities into dreamblocks that can be dreamed through.

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.triggerOnEnter: Whether to trigger this template when entering it from a non-dream state
placements.triggerOnLeave: Whether to trigger this template when exiting the dream state from it
placements.normalChannel: Entities in the template will behave/look normal when this channel is nonzero
placements.customVisualGroup: Specifies a custom visual group, to be used with the custom dreamlayer entity.
placements.useVisuals: Uncheck to make items inside appear as normal with no shader.
placements.allowTransition: Allows room transitions to start while dreaming in this block.
placements.tryDashhit: Attempts to resolve dashhit events of children before entering the block. Certain entities will stop you from using the dreamblock.
placements.sendDashhit: Send a dashhit event to parent templates when entering this block.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateEntity.description: Modify the visibility/collidability/activity of contained entities

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.activeChannel: If a channel is provided, contained entities will only be active when this channel is nonzero. The allowCustomSpeed checkbox allows non boolan speeds
placements.collidableChannel: If a channel is provided, contained entities will only be collidable when this channel is nonzero
placements.visibleChannel: If a channel is provided, contained entities will only be visible when this channel is nonzero.
placements.shakeChannel: If a channel is provided, setting it will cause contained entities to shake.
placements.allowCustomSpeed: If checked, can set the active channel to non boolean values. Entities inside will run at these numerical speeds. For example, setting the active channel to 0.5 will cause all contained entities to run at half speed.
placements.only: Only affects entities of the given type (found in the top bar of loenn entity menu, i.e. [modname]/[entityname])

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateFakewa.name: Template Fake Wall
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateFakewa.description: A fake wall that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.freeze: If this template's entities should update or not
dontOnTransitionInto: Whether this template should play the disappearing animation or not if the player transitions into this entity
placements.disappear_depth: The depth of this template as it's disappearing
placements.fade_speed: How fast this template disappears. (It takes 1/fadespeed seconds)
placements.persistent: Whether clearing this template permanantly stops it from appearing after respawn.
placements.caveMode: The items in this template will reappear when you no longer overlap them.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateFallin.name: Template Falling Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateFallin.description: A falling block that can be made with a template. All interior solids collide with world.

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.reverseChannel: If this channel is NOT 0, fall in the reverse direction
placements.triggerChannel: If this channel is NOT 0, make the template fall automatically
placements.gravity: How fast this template should accelerate (vanilla is 500)
placements.max_speed: The maximum speed this template should fall (vanilla is 160)
placements.impact_sfx: The sound to play when this template hits the ground
placements.shake_sfx: The sound to play when this template gets triggered
placements.set_trigger_channel: Set this channel to 1 when triggered
placements.hitJumpthrus: Whether jumpthrus should block this entity's movement. Works for sideways and upsidedown jumpthrus (and regular).
placements.triggeredByRiding: Whether riding triggers the fall sequence
placements.throughDashblocks: Whether to move through breakable blocks like dashblocks or breakable template blocks
placements.customFallTiming: A list of numbers. First number is delay after being triggered for first fall. Second is the delay used when support under the block is removed. The third is a cooldown started after impacting the ground.
placements.maxWaitTiming: How long the block delays falling when the player is grabbing it
placements.triggerOnImpact: Send a trigger event to parents when impacting the ground

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateGluabl.description: Attach a template to the movement of an entity

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.glue_to_identifier: The entity ID path to glue to. The number found after the ID: in loenn. Can also specify 'player' to glue to player.
placements.maxSpeed: The maximum speed this template can approach its target position with
placements.constraint: Constrain the entity's movement. For spline options, you must add nodes. Will get as close as possible to the entity without violating its constraint (i.e. changing X in an onlyY constraint or leaving the spline in a spline constraint)

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.sinkTime: How long it will sink for
placements.sinkDist: How far it will sink during its sink time
placements.respawnTime: How long it takes to respawn
placements.triggerable: Whether it will start sinking when triggered by child events
placements.ridingTriggers: Whether riding this entity triggers it
placements.disconnect: Whether to disconnect this template's movement from its parent when it starts sinking
placements.quiet: Various options to mute certain sounds. Can have value from 0-7 with each number muting a different variety of sounds. 7 mutes all.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateKevin.description: A kevin block that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.maxspeed: The top speed of this template
placements.returnSpeed: The return speed of this template
placements.left: Whether you can hit the left side
placements.right: Whether you can hit the right side
placements.top: Whether you can hit the top side
placements.bottom: Whether you can hit the bottom side
placements.hitJumpthrus: Whether jumpthrus should block this entity's movement. Works for sideways and upsidedown jumpthrus (and regular).
placements.throughDashblocks: Whether to move through breakable blocks like dashblocks or breakable template blocks

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateCloud.description: Make a template act like a cloud. Not sponsored. Ive never even used it in a map.

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.fragile: Whether to shatter at the end of the cloud boost thing
placements.respawnTime: The time it takes for the cloud to respawn
placements.cloudDir: If one value is specified, the vertical span of the cloud. If two values are specified, the first will be used for how much the cloud should move sideways (and the second for verticality)
placements.fromRiding: Whether riding this cloud triggers it
placements.noDoubleBoost: Disables double cloudboosts (jumping in coyote after the cloud has shattered) only important for clouds with horizontal direction.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateMoonbl.name: Template Moon Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateMoonbl.description: A moon block that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.drift_frequency: How fast the template bobs up and down
placements.drift_amplitude: How much distance the template bobs up and down
placements.sink_amount: How much distance the template sinks
placements.sink_speed: How fast the template sinks when stood on or grabbed
placements.dash_influence: How far a dash will push this block
placements.startphase: The floating phase this template should start on, in radians
placements.useCustomStartphase: If this template should start its floating on some certain phase. Check this if "Start Phase" is specified

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateMovebl.name: Template Move Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateMovebl.description: A move block that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.uncollidable_blocks: Even uncollidable blocks in this template will stop the block's movement when hitting something
placements.speed: The speed this template will move at
placements.acceleration: How fast this template will accelerate to its top speed
placements.respawning: Whether or not this template will respawn
placements.respawn_timer: How long it takes this template to respawn
placements.Max_stuck: How long this template can be stuck before it breaks
placements.cansteer: If the player can steer this template
placements.movesfx: The sound to play while this template is moving
placements.arrow_texture: Texture source to use. Should have subtextures 0-7, rotated as shown.
placements.decal_depth: Depth of the arrow graphics. Affected by depthoffset
placements.decal_colors: Takes two colors, the arrow color when moving and when still.
placements.max_leniency: The maximum amount of pixels this template can corner correct to avoid being stuck
placements.hitJumpthrus: Whether jumpthrus should block this entity's movement. Works for sideways and upsidedown jumpthrus (and regular).
placements.throughDashblocks: Whether to move through breakable blocks like dashblocks or breakable template blocks
placements.triggerFromRiding: Whether riding this template should trigger it (otherwise some trigger modified event must be used)

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplatePushBl.name: Template Push Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplatePushBl.description: A block that can be pushed with dashes or springs. Full world collision.

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.NoPhysicsTime: Gravity and drag disabled for this time period after dash hit
placements.speedFromDash: Speed gained when player dashes into this block
placements.horizontalDrag: How qukcly horizontal speed bleeds off
placements.movementLeniency: Will corner correct movement by up to this amount
placements.startDisconnected: Will immedately fall and not move with attached parent template
placements.hitSprings: Whether to be sprung by springs
placements.gravity: How quickly to approach terminal velocity. Can be thought of as vertical drag biased around a nonzero value.
placements.BounceStrengthFromWall: When hitting a wall, how much speed to recoil back with
placements.ownSpringRecoil: Speed gotten when entity uses a spring attached to this block (newton's third law)
placements.ImpactSfx: SOund to play when hits something
placements.hitJumpthrus: Whether jumpthrus should block this entity's movement. Works for sideways and upsidedown jumpthrus (and regular).
placements.throughDashblocks: Whether to move through breakable blocks like dashblocks or breakable template blocks

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateStatic.name: Template Staticmover
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateStatic.description: Attach a template to an existing solid

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.channel: The cassette material channel used for displaying this while inactive. If left blank, the template will be destroyed and remade every time the attached block enables/disables
placements.ridingTrigger: Whether riding this template should trigger what it is attached to (a-la spring on arrow block)
placements.EnableUnrooted: Whether this template can display without being attached to anything
placements.conveyRiding: Whether riding this template should count as riding what it is attached to
placements.triggerAsRiding: When triggered, will cause the platform it is attached to to register a rider, which is useful for compat with entities that have not implemented a triggered function.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateSwapbl.name: Template Swap Block
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateSwapbl.description: A swap block that can be made with a template

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.max_speed: The top speed of this template when it's swapping
placements.max_return_speed: The speed of this template when it's returning
placements.returning: If this template should act like a vanilla swap block or a toggle swap block
placements.spline: The spline type to use. Linear types move in straight lines. The catmull variants curve smoothly between nodes with sharp edges at knots (created by stacking two nodes on the same pixel)
placements.lastNodeIsKnot: Make the last node a knot even if it is not stacked. Can be disabled to allow for crooked paths on the last segment of a looping path. Having it on results in more intuitive behavior for new users.

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateTempla.description: Replace all occurances of various {KEY}'s in child entities with a new value

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.replacements: List of replacements to make. Specify replacements as KEY:Replacement or KEY. If no replacement is specified, a unique string will be generated. For example FISH:salmon,TIME will cause 'I ate {FISH} at {TIME}' to become 'I ate salmon at !24#9@1'

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateTrigge.description: A template wrapper that changes the way parent templates recieve trigger events from children

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.advancedTouchOptions: Touch events in this list will have the opposite behavior to triggerOnTouch. Works as either blacklist or whitelist.
placements.triggerOnTouch: Trigger when player touches, jumps off of, etc
placements.channel: When this channel is set to a nonzero value, trigger the template
placements.setChannel: Set this channel when this template is triggered. Can be given advanced input; a:3,b:@c[*2] sets channel a to 3 and channel b to twice the value of channel c.
placements.skipChannel: When this channel is set, triggering events will pass over this template without being modified
placements.propagateRiding: Child zipmovers/falling blocks will trigger from this being stood on
placements.propagateDashHit: Whether dashhits go through this template
placements.propagateTrigger: Whether to pass trigger events to parents
placements.hideTrigger: Whether to hide trigger events (preventing parents from triggering.) Events are unhidden at next trigger modifier
placements.delay: Delay all trigger events by this amount.
placements.blockFilter: Works as a comma seperated white/blacklist. Trigger categories on this list have opposite behavior to the blockTrigger box. Can end strings with * for fuzzy matching
placements.log: Log any trigger types to the debug console. Used to determine what to put in white/blacklist

entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateZipmov.name: Template Zip Mover
entities.auspicioushelper/TemplateZipmov.description: A zip mover that can be made with a template. Supports multiple nodes and multiple stops. To add a stop use two stacked nodes

placements.template: The name of the template to use. (<room>/<name>). If left empty, can be used in behavior chains and in connected tiles.
placements.depthoffset: How much to offset the depth of contained entities. Accumulates from parent to child.
placements.return_type: The behavior this template has when reaching the last node
placements.channel: Channel to use for grouped template zipmover movement (all zipmovers on the same channel trigger eachother)
placements.propegateRiding: Whether to tell parent templates if this has a rider
placements.spline: The spline type to use. Linear types move in straight lines. The catmull variants curve smoothly between nodes with sharp edges at knots (created by stacking two nodes on the same pixel)
placements.lastNodeIsKnot: Make the last node a knot even if it is not stacked. Can be disabled to allow for crooked paths on the last segment of a looping path. Having it on results in more intuitive behavior for new users.
placements.speed: How fast to move. The outgoing journey will take 1/speed seconds.
placements.returnSpeed: How fast to return. The return will take 1/returnSpeed seconds.
placements.easing: Easing function to use when outgoing
